official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state

official_functions.function_or_member: ClientboundAddEntityPacket(int, UUID, double, double, double, float, float, EntityType<?>, int, Vec3, double)
official_functions.role: Construct the add_entity packet fixture without initialized Entity or ServerEntity state
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound add_entity frame
Confirm the fixture entity type registry id, zero movement body bytes, and listener surface

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns add_entity immediately after bundle_delimiter.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the add_entity id to ClientboundAddEntityPacket.
The official add_entity body order is entity id, UUID, entity type registry id, position doubles, Vec3.LP movement, x rotation byte, y rotation byte, y-head rotation byte, and data VarInt.
The fixture uses bootstrapped built-in EntityType.PIG and Vec3.ZERO so it does not require initialized world, Entity, or ServerEntity state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded field values, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove arbitrary entity registry contents, initialized Entity/ServerEntity behavior, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer arbitrary entity registry contents, initialized Entity/ServerEntity behavior, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
